dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a Sick Person.
Hello, I'm EMT Alex, part of the rescue squad. What led you to call 911?
I've been feeling nauseous and vomiting since yesterday evening. My last meal was around lunchtime, and the vomit looked like water with some bile.
Are you feeling dizzy or otherwise unwell aside from the vomiting?
Do you have existing medical conditions or take any medications?
I do have primary hypertension and a history of alcohol use. I have no allergies and am not on any medications.
Would you say you're in any kind of pain right now?
I'm not in pain at the moment.
We'll note your pain as zero for now.
On a scale from Alert to Unresponsive, how are you feeling right now?
Can you open your eyes without help?
Could you share your name, today's date, and where you are?
When you're ready, can you move your fingers and toes as I ask?
Your airway is clear, and I can't see any signs of blockage.
You're breathing regularly and spontaneously, no distress noted.
I didn't notice any bleeding, and your skin is pink, warm, and not overly wet.
Can you share more about the vomiting-how it started or how it felt?
Any food or drug allergies?
You're not currently taking medications, is that correct?
You mentioned chronic hypertension and alcohol dependence. Have you experienced any worsening recently?
You said your last meal was lunchtime yesterday. Have you had any fluids or food since then?
The vomiting began at about 4:00 PM yesterday. Was there anything different that happened around that time?
Your chest looks normal, symmetric and without any trauma.
Your head is normal with no deformities or signs of injury.
I see no drainage or signs of trauma in your ears, nose, or throat.
Your heart is beating fast but otherwise normal. I don't see any murmurs or extra heart sounds.
I don't see any tenderness, guarding, or bulges in your abdomen.
No irregularities or instability were found with your pelvis.
I see no symptoms or signs of genitourinary involvement.
Your arms and legs show no deformities, and your pulses are intact.
No bruising, injuries, or issues were seen on your back or spine.
Your coordination and reflexes are functioning normally based on neurological assessment.
Your skin looks normal-dry with good turgor and no signs of rashes or injury.
Your heart rate was initially 127 beats per minute and later slowed to 111.
Your breathing rate has been steady at 18 breaths per minute.
Your blood pressure initially read 195 over 109, and later came down to 169 over 113.
Your first glucose reading was 156, but it couldn't be measured again.
Both oxygen levels we saw were good, 97% and 95%.
The EKG continues to show sinus tachycardia.
You're fully alert and oriented with a Glasgow score of 15.
We've placed an IV into your left forearm.
We gave you 4 mg of Zofran through the IV, which gave moderate relief.
You are being monitored with a 4-lead ECG per the medical protocol for vomiting.
We're using the medical protocol for vomiting and nausea (Protocol 3 - 9) based on your status.
As directed by protocol and your request, we're taking you to [COMPANY_NAME].
I saw no signs of jugular venous distention.
You'll be seated and strapped in safely for our transport. Let us know if anything changes.
You've now been moved to ED Room 6 at [COMPANY_NAME]. We've given the information to the nurse.
You remain alert and oriented throughout the evaluation and treatment process.
Reassessments found no new bleeding or distress.
Your latest vitals are stable with no major changes.
You didn't report new symptoms, but did mention something about your alcohol use history.
Your IV is in place and the hospital team handled your ECG. We'll monitor you while you're taken into the hospital.
Your IV remains in place, and we are done with transport setup.
You're now moving into the emergency department with a little help from our team.
Your chest is atraumatic and appears normal from front to back.
I observed no tracheal deviation or signs of airway displacement.
Both upper and lower extremities are unremarkable and show normal function.
Your ears, nose, and mouth remain clean and show no abnormalities.
We were unable to obtain a second glucose measurement, but the first is noted in documentation.
Any sudden changes in your condition during transit should be reported immediately.
No palpable pulses or murmurs were noted on the common carotid artery.
What kinds of drinks have you had to connect with the timing of your nausea onset?
We looked again-no jugular venous distention is present.
Your skin shows no signs of defacement or irritation on primary inspection.
The airway remains patent with no signs of anatomic airway displacement.
Both pupils are equal and reactive to light and accommodation.
The EKG mirrored sinus tachycardia across both measurement periods without complications.
We will transport you right to the entrance with no detours or delays.
There are no signs of instability or injury in the pelvic region.
Your ossification centers and hippocampal functions show no current impairment.
A tactile assessment revealed no pain, tenderness in your upper or lower back.
You are fully supported and the transport padding is intact and secure.
Both heart rate and blood pressure were trending toward resolution with baseline.
Your documentation has been transferred over. You've arrived at active hospital care.
No signs of sinus pressure or nasal obstruction were found on inspection.
Your heart sounds were heard clearly without areas of accentuation or murmurs.
Each physician's note confirms you were properly handed over as advised by EMS training.
Your awareness and response have remained the same with continued treatment.
No abnormalities arose in observation or documentation of vitals centrally or peripherally.
No lack of use or angulation was found in either upper or lower extremities.
Your skin surface maintains even turgor with no signs of abnormal folding or elasticity.
The intravenous line remained patent and functional throughout treatment and transport.
The second blood pressure reading showed signs of reduction status from initial observation.
No changes in movement were observed in your position after securing to the stretcher.
We arrived by policy and your stated request at the designated facility without delay.
We note a reduction in discomfort as a positive outcome following opioid-free management.
No crepitus or instability was felt during the pelvic inspection.
No abnormal sensory or motor function was found in upper or lower body.
The IV remains stable and fully under control by facility nursing as we complete the log.
ED staff have access to all documentation and they are ready for onsite care.
You are now considered under control of the hospital and we've unlinked from your case.
Blood pressure, pulse, and oxygen levels continue to remain within expected ranges.
You were noted to have good extraocular movement and lack of ocular fatigue.
We confirmed symmetry and full range of motion for your extremities during inspection.
Active monitoring of your condition is maintained until you are moved to the hospital bed.
Chest movement remains symmetrical with no mechanical difficulties observed.
We also noted no signs of fluid retention on the neck or clavicular region.
No signs of heart failure or arrhythmic activity were noted based on inspection.
No signs of shingles, psoriasis, or demodex were observed in your skin assessment.
